articles.title: Governance and organisation
articles.description: Internal governance and control framework for effective ICT risk management. Management body defines, approves, and oversees implementation. Ultimate responsibility with management body.

articles.title: ICT risk management framework
articles.description: Sound, comprehensive, well-documented framework comprising strategies, policies, procedures, ICT protocols, and tools. Reviewed at least annually.

articles.title: ICT systems, protocols and tools
articles.description: Use and maintain updated ICT systems appropriate to scale, adequate to support critical functions. Must be reliable, have sufficient capacity, and be technologically resilient.

articles.title: Identification
articles.description: Identify, classify, and document all ICT-supported business functions, information assets, and ICT assets. Identify all sources of ICT risk. Maintain ICT asset inventory.

articles.title: Protection and prevention
articles.description: Continuously monitor and control ICT systems security. Implement IAM, strong authentication, network security, encryption, data protection policies. ICT security awareness programmes.

articles.title: Detection
articles.description: Mechanisms to promptly detect anomalous activities. Multiple layers of control, alert thresholds, and incident response initiation.

articles.title: Response and recovery
articles.description: ICT business continuity policy and response/recovery plans. Estimate impact and activate plans when incidents occur.

articles.title: Backup policies and restoration
articles.description: Backup policies with scope, frequency, retention. Restoration must not jeopardise integrity/confidentiality. Recovery plans tested periodically. Redundant ICT capacities.

articles.title: Learning and evolving
articles.description: Gather and analyse information on vulnerabilities, cyber threats, ICT incidents. Post-incident reviews mandatory. Findings fed into risk assessment and training.

articles.title: Communication
articles.description: Communication plans for internal and external stakeholders. At least one designated person for public/media communication on ICT incidents.

articles.title: Further harmonisation
articles.description: ESAs develop common regulatory technical standards to further specify ICT risk management framework elements.

articles.title: Simplified ICT risk management
articles.description: Certain entities meeting specific criteria may apply simplified framework. Core requirements retained but proportionally adjusted.

articles.title: Incident management process
articles.description: Define, establish, and implement ICT incident management process for detection, management, and notification. Record all significant cyber threats.

articles.title: Classification of incidents
articles.description: Classify ICT incidents and determine impact based on: clients affected, duration, geographical spread, data losses, criticality of services, economic impact.

articles.title: Reporting major incidents
articles.description: Report major ICT incidents to competent authority. Initial notification, intermediate report, and final report within specified timeframes.

articles.title: Harmonisation of reporting
articles.description: ESAs develop common reporting content and templates for consistency across the financial sector.

articles.title: Centralisation of reporting
articles.description: ESAs shall develop joint draft regulatory technical standards to establish a single EU Hub for major ICT-related incident reporting by financial entities.

articles.title: Supervisory feedback
articles.description: Competent authorities shall provide feedback and guidance to financial entities following notification of major ICT-related incidents, including anonymised threat intelligence sharing.

articles.title: Payment-related operational or security incidents
articles.description: Credit institutions, payment institutions, account information service providers, and e-money institutions shall also report payment-related operational or security incidents previously reported under PSD2 to the competent authority under DORA.

articles.title: General testing requirements
articles.description: Establish, maintain, and review sound digital operational resilience testing programme as integral part of ICT risk management framework.

articles.title: Testing of ICT tools and systems
articles.description: Vulnerability assessments, open source analyses, network security assessments, gap analyses, physical security reviews, source code reviews, penetration testing.

articles.title: Advanced testing (TLPT)
articles.description: Threat-Led Penetration Testing at least every 3 years. Must cover critical functions, be on live production, performed by qualified external testers.

articles.title: Requirements for TLPT testers
articles.description: External testers must be of highest suitability, possess technical capabilities, be certified, and carry professional indemnity insurance.

articles.title: General principles
articles.description: Manage ICT third-party risk as integral component. Remain fully responsible for compliance. Adopt ICT third-party risk strategy. Maintain register of contractual arrangements.

articles.title: ICT concentration risk assessment
articles.description: Identify and assess whether contractual arrangement creates ICT concentration risk, dependency, or sub-outsourcing chains.

articles.title: Key contractual provisions
articles.description: Contracts must include: service descriptions, SLDs, processing locations, data security provisions, termination rights, audit rights, incident notification, and exit strategies.
